mechanism_narrative: A lower FEDFUNDS rate signals easier financial conditions that reduce the discount rate on equities and support risk appetite, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when policy is not tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in a majority of months when FEDFUNDS is falling for at least 2 consecutive observations, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals deteriorating labor income and higher recession risk, which lowers expected corporate earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes deliver positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time, or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 standard error.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates while PAYEMS is stable or improving, the data imply stronger household spending and revenue growth, which should lift S&P 500 prices over the next 5 trading days through near-term earnings revision optimism.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition RSAFS rising with PAYEMS flat-to-up does not improve 5-day forward returns versus either series alone, or if the interaction term is not positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp fall in INDPRO combined with elevated UNRATE signals supply and labor slack from an economic contraction, which raises recession odds and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average by 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if periods with INDPRO below its 6-month average and UNRATE elevated do not show materially negative 60-day returns, or if the signal's out-of-sample hit rate is below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in HOUST reflects improving housing demand and construction activity, which leads aggregate demand and supports cyclical earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if rising HOUST does not precede above-baseline 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if the sign of the rolling 3-year slope between HOUST changes and forward returns is negative.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains below inflation, real policy rates fall and financial conditions ease, so equities re-rate upward over the next 20 trading days as liquidity expectations improve.
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-above-policy-rate condition does not correspond to positive 20-day returns, or if the interaction effect disappears after controlling for level changes in FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS series indicates job losses and weakening household income, which should depress consumer-sensitive earnings expectations and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines for 2 consecutive monthly releases
failure_condition: Reject if two-month employment declines are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns more often than negative returns, or if the signal's information coefficient is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in GDPC1 and RSAFS identifies broad demand expansion that raises expected sales and earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDP and retail sales strength does not outperform single-series signals on 60-day returns, or if the combined effect is not positive in expanding-regime samples.
